title: Artist
content:
Quit the hut, frequent the palace,
    Reck not what the people say;
    For still, where'er the trees grow biggest,
    Huntsmen find the easiest way.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Ralph Waldo Emerson, titled "Artist", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
